title: Thermal control of the seismogenic zone of southern central Chile
journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth
abstract: Thermal models for the Chile subduction zone are developed along two profiles at 38.2°S and 42°S within the rupture area of the 1960 Valdivia earthquake and south of the 2010 Maule earthquake. The age difference of the subducting Nazca Plate has a major impact on the thermal regime. Seafloor heat flow values derived from the depth of gas hydrate bottom-simulating reflectors confirm that the southern area has higher heat flow due to the younger, hotter incoming plate. The seismogenic zone widens and shifts landward to greater depths from south to north.

location.description: South-central Chile subduction zone at two profiles: 38.2°S (TIPTEQ line) and 42°S

data.name: SPOC/TIPTEQ MCS Profiles — BSR Heat Flow at 38.2°S and 42°S
data.description: MCS reflection profiles from SPOC (SO161) and TIPTEQ (SO181) used to map BSR depth for heat flow estimation at 38.2°S and 42°S; confirms higher heat flow at 42°S from younger incoming plate. [TIPTEQ cruise (SO181, 2004/2005) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

data.name: Navigation:Primary
data.description: acquired during the Robert D. Conrad expedition RC2901 (1988)

_data_note: JGR paper; behind paywall. Uses SPOC/TIPTEQ data from MGDS for BSR-based thermal modeling.

processing.summary: BSR depths from MCS reflection profiles at 38.2°S (TIPTEQ line, SO181) and 42°S (SPOC line, SO161) are used to derive seafloor heat flow values for thermal modeling of the seismogenic zone. The thermal models show that the younger (~3 Ma) plate at 42°S produces higher heat flow than the older (~14 Ma) plate at 38.2°S, resulting in a narrower and shallower seismogenic zone in the south.
workflow: 1. BSR depth mapping from MCS profiles
2. BSR-to-heat-flow conversion using gas hydrate stability models
3. Thermal finite element modeling of subduction zones
4. Comparison of modeled and BSR-derived heat flow
processing.notes: Thermal modeling study using BSR depth from existing MCS profiles as heat flow proxy

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF. 5 authors confirmed correct. vessel: added R/V Conrad RC2901 (profiles c727, c728, c732 cited in Fig 1 for 38.2°S and 42°S transects). year_acquired: 2001→1988 (earliest data, RC2901). Thermal model uses BSR depths from SPOC SO161, TIPTEQ SO181, and R/V Conrad RC2901 profiles.
